content.text:
Database Architecture and Administration
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF21  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Understand the architecture of a DBMS and how it manages a database. Acquire the skills and know-how of a Database Administrator.
Prerequisites:
Initial notions of databases: design, creation, and use of a database.
Course Content (21h)
Chapter 1 – DBMS Architecture
Software architecture: layers, components
Internal processes of a DBMS
Client-server and multi-user model
Centralised vs distributed architecture
Chapter 2 – Installation and Configuration
DBMS selection (Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL...)
Installation and environment configuration
Database creation and initialisation
Management of data and control files
Chapter 3 – Storage Management
Physical and logical structures
Tablespaces, data files, segments
Indexing and performance management
Chapter 4 – Security and Access Control
User accounts, roles, privileges
Authentication, logging, audit
Connection security and encryption
Chapter 5 – Concurrency and Transactions
ACID transactions
Locking, isolation, anomalies
Optimistic and pessimistic concurrency control
Chapter 6 – Backup and Recovery
Logical vs physical backup
Full, incremental, differential backup
Recovery from partial or total failure
Chapter 7 – Optimisation and Tuning
Execution plan and query analysis
Index and join optimisation
Statistics, performance analysis
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
Bibliography
Elmasri, R., & Navathe, S. B. (2017).
Fundamentals of Database Systems
(7th ed.). Pearson.
Silberschatz, A., Korth, H. F., & Sudarshan, S. (2020).
Database System Concepts
(7th ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Garcia-Molina, H., Ullman, J. D., & Widom, J. (2008).
Database Systems: The Complete Book
(2nd ed.). Pearson.
Connolly, T., & Begg, C. (2015).
Database Systems: A Practical Approach
(6th ed.). Pearson.
Desai, B. C. (2012).
An Introduction to Database Systems
. Galgotia Publications.
